home.title: 16 Personality Types Test
home.subtitle: A personality test based on Jung's theory of psychological types
home.disclaimer: This is not an official MBTI test. It is independently developed based on Jung's theory of psychological types.

quiz.pickInstruction: Choose the most natural option for you

result.celebrityDisclaimer: * Based on publicly available information; not personally confirmed.

compatibility.pageSubtitle: Select two types and check their compatibility

q1.question: How do you recharge on weekends?

options.o1: Relax alone at home to recharge
options.o2: Meet up with friends and chat
options.o3: Enjoy a book or movie at a cafe

q2.question: When you arrive at a new social gathering, what do you do?

options.o1: Walk up and introduce yourself first
options.o2: Wait for someone to approach you
options.o3: Stay close to someone you know
options.o4: Take the lead and drive conversations

options.o1: Listen to music alone or take a walk
options.o2: Call a friend and meet up
options.o3: Write in a journal or organize thoughts alone
options.o4: Talk it out with several people

options.o1: Lots of team projects and meetings
options.o2: A place where I can work independently
options.o4: A place that allows remote work

q5.question: How do you plan a trip?

options.o2: Just mark must-see spots and go with the flow
options.o3: Plan around popular restaurants and attractions
options.o4: Wander around and get inspired by the locale

q6.question: What's your preferred way of learning something new?

options.o1: Follow the textbook step by step
options.o4: Understand the theory and reasoning first

q7.question: What do you usually talk about in conversations?

options.o3: Apply proven methods step by step

q9.question: When a friend comes to you with a problem, what do you do?

options.o2: Empathize and listen to their feelings
options.o3: List the pros and cons for them
options.o4: Share in their emotions -- laugh and cry together

q10.question: When making an important decision, what matters most?

options.o2: The feelings and impact on people involved
options.o4: Finding a way that satisfies everyone

q11.question: When conflict arises in a team project, what do you do?

options.o1: Focus on the goal and set emotions aside
options.o2: Check on team members' feelings first
options.o3: Resolve it according to rules and standards
options.o4: Try to understand each other through dialogue

q12.question: What movie genre are you drawn to?

q13.question: How do you go about your daily routine?

options.o2: Go with how I feel in the moment
options.o3: Make a to-do list and check things off

options.o2: Peak focus right before the deadline
options.o3: Break it into stages and work through them
options.o4: Need the pressure to get started

q15.question: How do you organize your closet or desk?

q16.question: How do you react when plans suddenly change?

options.o1: It stresses me out and feels uncomfortable

ENFP.title: The Enthusiastic Campaigner
ENFP.description: A free spirit who is creative and passionate. Excellent at discovering new possibilities and inspiring others.

INTJ.title: The Strategic Mastermind
INTJ.description: An independent and analytical strategist. Holds high standards and excels at solving complex problems.

INFP.title: The Dreamy Mediator
INFP.description: An idealistic mediator with outstanding empathy. Values inner convictions and strives to make the world a better place.

ESTJ.title: The Strict Executive
ESTJ.description: A systematic and practical manager. Values rules and order, and acts with strong responsibility and decisiveness.

ISFJ.title: The Brave Defender
ISFJ.description: A warm and dedicated defender. Finds fulfillment in helping others, and is attentive and trustworthy.

ENTP.title: The Bold Debater
ENTP.description: A witty and challenging debater. Overflowing with intellectual curiosity and loves breaking conventions.

ISFP.title: The Curious Adventurer
ISFP.description: A quiet and sensitive artist. Views the world through personal values and pursues beauty.

ENTJ.title: The Bold Commander
ENTJ.description: A charismatic leader. Values efficiency and results, and excels at turning big visions into reality.

INTP.title: The Logical Thinker
INTP.description: A curious thinker. Enjoys logical analysis and theoretical exploration, and produces original ideas.

ESFP.title: The Free-Spirited Entertainer
ESFP.description: An energetic and fun-loving social personality. Gives energy to those around them and lives in the moment.

ISTP.title: The Versatile Virtuoso
ISTP.description: A logical yet practical personality. Excels with hands-on skills and solving problems on the spot.

ESFJ.title: The Social Consul
ESFJ.description: A warm and caring personality. Values harmony and excels at looking after others.

INFJ.title: The Insightful Advocate
INFJ.description: An insightful and idealistic personality. Understands others through deep empathy and pursues meaningful change.

ENFJ.title: The Passionate Protagonist
ENFJ.description: A leader who combines charisma and empathy. Passionate about helping others grow and inspiring people.

ISTJ.title: The Reliable Logistician
ISTJ.description: A trustworthy and diligent personality. Values tradition and rules, and has a strong sense of duty.

ESTP.title: The Adventurous Entrepreneur
ESTP.description: An energetic and action-oriented personality. Embraces risk and excels at solving real-world problems.

fallback.title: Type {type}
fallback.description: You are type {type}. You have a unique personality combination.

EI: Similar energy direction means your lifestyles sync well
You have similar expectations for social activities
SN: You process information the same way, making communication easy
TF: Similar decision-making styles lead to fewer conflicts
You share similar values and judgment criteria
JP: Your lifestyle patterns and planning styles match well
You handle tasks in a similar way

EI: Extroversion meets introversion for a balanced social life
Different energy directions provide diverse perspectives
SN: Practical and visionary perspectives complement each other
You can see both the details and the big picture together
TF: Logic and emotion combine for better decisions
Rational analysis and empathy complement each other
JP: Structure meets flexibility for greater adaptability
You find balance between organization and freedom

EI: You'll need to balance alone time and together time
Understanding different expectations for social activities is key
SN: Recognize and respect differences in communication styles
Understand the gap between fact-focused and possibility-focused thinking
TF: Respect each other's different ways of expressing emotions
Balance logic and emotion when making decisions together
JP: Be considerate about flexibility when plans change
Compromise between deadlines and freedom is needed

score5: Appreciate each other's strengths and enjoy the natural flow of the relationship.
You already match well, but don't take each other for granted.
Your differences actually enrich the relationship.
score4: View your differences as complementary strengths, not weaknesses.
An open mind to learning from each other deepens the relationship.
Respecting small differences creates great synergy.
score3: Build on your similarities while working to understand your differences.
Listening to each other's perspectives improves the relationship.
Find shared interests and spend time together.
score2: Acknowledge the differences and look for what you can learn from each other.
Patience and effort to understand each other's ways can lead to significant growth.
Clear communication is key to reducing misunderstandings.
score1: Completely different perspectives can open new worlds. Keep an open mind.
Respect each other's differences and start by finding small commonalities.
Try to find the differences between you fascinating rather than frustrating.

sameTypeStrengths: You can instantly understand each other's way of thinking
You share similar life rhythms and values
sameTypeChallenges: Sharing the same weaknesses makes it hard to compensate for each other
Similar thinking styles may cause you to miss new perspectives
compatData.noChallenges: There are few sources of major conflict in this relationship

descriptions.same: Two {type1}s ({title1}) can deeply understand each other. You share similar values and thinking styles, but be aware that you may also share the same weaknesses -- consciously compensating for them is important.
descriptions.score5: {type1} ({title1}) and {type2} ({title2}) naturally complement each other -- a perfect match. Different strengths create synergy, and being together helps both grow into better versions of themselves.
descriptions.score4: {type1} ({title1}) and {type2} ({title2}) fill in each other's gaps nicely. Slight differences actually make the relationship more interesting and provide new perspectives.
descriptions.score3: {type1} ({title1}) and {type2} ({title2}) share some commonalities and some differences. With effort to understand each other, you can build a stable and balanced relationship.
descriptions.score2: {type1} ({title1}) and {type2} ({title2}) differ in many ways, but that means there's much to learn from each other. With patience and understanding, you can grow together.
descriptions.score1: {type1} ({title1}) and {type2} ({title2}) are opposites in nearly every way. It may be hard to understand at first, but accepting each other's differences can lead to the greatest personal growth.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.section1Text: "16 Personality Types Test" (hereinafter "Service") is a free web service that analyzes users' personality types. This Service does not require registration and does not separately collect or store users' personal information.
privacy.section2Text: This Service does not store test response data on any server. All test processing is done in the user's browser (client-side), and test results are generated only on the user's device.
privacy.section3Text: This Service may use Google Analytics to understand website usage patterns. Google Analytics collects the following anonymized information through cookies:
section3List: Traffic source (search engine, direct access, etc.)
privacy.section3Suffix: Collected data is processed in a non-personally identifiable form and managed according to Google's Privacy Policy. For details, see
privacy.section4Text: This Service may use cookies for the following purposes:
privacy.section4Suffix: You can block cookies through browser settings, but some service features may be limited.
privacy.section5Text: This Service may display ads through Google AdSense. Google AdSense may use cookies to serve interest-based ads. For details, see
privacy.section6Text: If you have questions about this Privacy Policy, please contact us at the address below.
privacy.section7Text: This Privacy Policy may be updated to reflect changes in law or service. Changes will be announced on this page.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.section1Text: "16 Personality Types Test" (hereinafter "Service") is a free web service that analyzes users' personality types through simple questions. By using this Service, you agree to the following terms.
terms.section2Text1: Test results provided by this Service are for entertainment and reference purposes only. This test does not replace professional psychological diagnosis, counseling, or medical evaluation, and should not be treated as a professional psychological assessment.
terms.section2Text2: This Service is not responsible for any decisions or actions based on test results. If you have psychological concerns, please consult a professional counselor or psychologist.
terms.section3Text1: MBTI (Myers-Briggs Type Indicator) is a registered trademark of The Myers-Briggs Company. This Service is not an official MBTI test and has no affiliation, sponsorship, or certification from The Myers-Briggs Company.
terms.section3Text2: This Service is an independent personality type analysis based on Carl Jung's theory of personality types.
terms.section4Text: Celebrity personality type information provided by this Service is based on publicly available sources and may not have been personally confirmed by those individuals. This information is provided for reference only.
terms.section5Text: All intellectual property of this Service, including design, content, and code, belongs to the service operator. Users may share test results for personal purposes, but may not commercially reproduce, distribute, or modify the Service's content without permission.
terms.section6Text: This Service is provided free of charge and may display ads through Google AdSense and other services to cover operating costs. Ad content does not represent this Service's views.
terms.section7Text: The service operator may change or suspend the Service without prior notice, and is not liable for any resulting damages.
terms.section8Text: These terms may be updated for service improvements or legal changes. Changes will be announced on this page.
